data.title: ZAWYA-SNG: Saudi Arabia: STC Bank announces collaboration deal with Mastercard
data.description:
Staff Writer

Mastercard has announced a collaboration with STC Bank, a subsidiary of state-owned Saudi Telecom Company, to empower its customers in the kingdom – both consumers and businesses – with greater access to a broad range of cross-border payment services, leveraging Mastercard Move’s innovative money movement capabilities.

In line with Vision 2030, the collaboration is fully geared towards transforming the Saudi payments landscape by accelerating the digital transformation of Saudi financial services in accordance with growing digital adoption and consumers’ changing preferences, said the statement from Mastercard.

Leveraging Mastercard Move - Mastercard’s portfolio of money movement capabilities - the collaboration will enable STC Bank’s customers to benefit from fast, secure, reliable, and cost-efficient digital cross-border transfers from Saudi Arabia to 120 countries - meeting the needs of consumers and businesses across the country in search of fast and flexible virtual payments solutions.

Mastercard Move provides banks and non-bank financial institutions with fast, secure money movement solutions, both domestically and internationally. 

The portfolio of solutions reaches more than 200 countries and territories and 150+ currencies, with access to more than 95% of the world’s banked population, it stated.

"There is a clear and immediate need for responsive solutions that enable consumers to access fast, secure and reliable payments, wherever and however they choose," said Onur Kursun, Executive Vice President, Commercial and New Payment Flows, Eastern Europe, Middle East and Africa (EEMEA), Mastercard. 

"We are pleased to have joined hands with STC Bank to facilitate such payments experiences for STC Bank customers. With Mastercard Move, we open the door to the future of payments, enabling people and businesses to send and receive money with confidence and ease," he stated.

Mohammad Refaie, Acting Chief Commercial Officer in STC Bank, said: "As part of our mission to serve as Saudi Arabia’s leading digital bank, we are fully committed to transforming financial services in the Kingdom and wider region."

"Our collaboration with Mastercard has been launched with the goal of offering access to innovative cross-border payments solutions that reshape the Kingdom’s economic landscape for the better," he noted.

"Crucially, Mastercard’s Borderless Payments research shows that people sending money abroad continue to prioritise speed, transparency and ease of use. More than one in three senders said their families had limited access points to receive funds, reinforcing the need for a wider range of payout options such as bank accounts, mobile wallets, cards and cash," he noted. 

Mastercard's collaboration with STC Bank directly supports these needs by expanding secure, reliable and easily accessible cross-border payment services, ensuring people can receive money in the way that works best for them, he added.

data.title: Exail shares slump as bond issue induces short-term pressure
data.description:
** Shares of Exail Technologies fall close to 7% after it launched a 200 million euro ($233.22 million) issue of convertible bonds

** TP ICAP MidCap analyst Julien Thomas flags a dilution overhang coming from the French underwater drone maker's deal

** The deal could trigger additional net short positions linked to hedging, weighing on the shares near-term, Thomas adds

** The deal was done "taking advantage of the high price of the bonds issued in September," and "should not be read" as a signal on its talks with British credit investor ICG , the analyst says

($1 = 0.8576 euros)

data.title: Global central bankers defend Fed's Powell after Trump threat
data.description:
The chiefs of many of the world's major central banks issued a joint statement in support of Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell on Tuesday after the Trump administration threatened him with a criminal indictment.

"We stand in full solidarity with the Federal Reserve System and its Chair Jerome H. Powell," the heads of the European Central Bank, the Bank of England and nine other institutions said.

"The independence of central banks is a cornerstone of price, financial and economic stability in the interest of the citizens that we serve," they added.

The U.S. administration's criminal probe is formally about the renovation of the Fed's headquarters but Powell called it a "pretext" to win presidential influence over interest rates.

data.title: Eurozone's Household Saving Rate Declined in Third Quarter
data.description:
By Ed Frankl

The eurozone's saving rate fell in the third quarter of last year, helping support the economy as spending modestly recovered.

The household saving rate decreased to 15.1% in the three months through September from 15.4% in the second quarter, the European Union's statistics agency Eurostat said Tuesday. Consumption increased at a faster rate than gross disposable income, it said.

Savings between April and June equaled their highest rate since the second quarter of 2021, the data showed. The rate spiked during the pandemic, as households held back spending when businesses closed.

Reducing the saving rate is an important way to boost activity, especially so as European economies are relying more on domestic demand after tariffs and persistent geopolitical concerns dragged on exports in 2025.

"Real incomes are set to rise further and the saving rate should gradually come down from its still high level, supporting consumption," the European Central Bank's President Christine Lagarde said in December.

The ECB said it expected the saving rate to average 15.0% in 2025, before edging down to 14.7% in both 2026 and 2027.

However, interest rates remain above historical levels despite having come down in recent years, and access to credit continues to be tight, meaning an elevated saving ratio is set to endure, the ECB noted in a December report.

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com

data.title: Stocks rise as upcoming US inflation data eclipses geopolitics, Fed worries
data.description:
Key points:
  • Stocks rise even with multiple geopolitical risks
  • Yen slide helps dollar steady as Fed concerns linger
  • Gold rally pauses near $4,600, oil up on Iran troubles
  • Earnings season kicks off with big U.S. banks
  • US consumer inflation data due later

By Amanda Cooper

Global shares rose on Tuesday and the dollar recovered some lost ground ahead of U.S. inflation data and major bank earnings, while oil prices gained as unrest in Iran fanned fears for supplies.

U.S. President Donald Trump's pledge on Monday to slap a 25% tariff rate on any country that does business with Iran, along with his attacks on Federal Reserve independence, added to market uncertainty, keeping investors cautious.

Europe's STOXX 600 was up just 0.1%, while U.S. stock index futures , were mostly flat.

In Asia, Japan's Nikkei returned from holiday with a jump of 3.3% to record highs, aided by a slide in the yen to historic lows and much talk of fiscal stimulus.

Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi plans to dissolve parliament to call an early election, Kyodo news agency reported, as she hopes to bolster her coalition's parliamentary majority, which would provide scope for more aggressive policies.

WALL OF WORRY

Even with multiple geopolitical flashpoints, such as Iran, Greenland and Venezuela, and concern about lofty valuations as equity benchmarks from London to Tokyo and Frankfurt hit record highs, investors appear content to push markets higher for now, a dynamic strategists call "climbing the wall of worry".

"The wall of worry has been built anew over the last week in so many different ways. And yet, (the market) is just climbing and climbing," IG chief market strategist Chris Beauchamp said.

"If people came into 2026 the same way they finished 2025, mainly they're looking for the pullback, they're looking for a reason for the market to correct. At the moment, it feels like they're just running the old hits of tariffs and interventions," he said.

A cornerstone of some of the current confidence is anticipated earnings growth of 8.3% in the fourth quarter, which would mark the 10th straight quarter of expansion, Beauchamp added.

"That's your fuel in the engine," he said.

Traders and investors are waiting for a report on U.S. consumer price inflation for December. Forecasts are for annual core inflation to nudge up to 2.7%.

Earnings season kicks off this week with results from the major banks including JPMorgan Chase , Bank of New York Mellon , Citigroup and Bank of America .

Earnings calls will no doubt include questions about banks' response to Trump's call for a one-year cap on credit card interest rates at 10% starting on January 20.

The banks have already warned such a step could result in millions of American households and small businesses losing access to credit, essentially a tightening in monetary policy.

GOLD SHINES THROUGH UNCERTAINTY

Three former Fed Chairs, Janet Yellen, Ben Bernanke and Alan Greenspan, signed a statement on Monday decrying the assault on the central bank's independence by the Trump administration. They warned that this is more typical in "emerging economies with weak institutions" and can have highly negative consequences for inflation.

Global central bank officials issued a coordinated statement of support for Fed Chair Jerome Powell on Tuesday after the Trump administration threatened him with a criminal indictment.

The dollar held relatively firm against a basket of currencies , having lost 0.25% overnight. The euro was flat at $1.1665 , while the pound rose 0.1% to $1.3477.

The yen came under heavy fire, falling to an all-time low against the euro and Swiss franc, which in turn helped boost the dollar by 0.5% on the day to 158.89 , sparking another round of verbal warnings from Japanese officials that can often precede intervention.

Gold, meanwhile, topped $4,600 an ounce, having hit a record high of $4,629.94 on Monday.

"Gold serves as a catch-all, and a default hedge of last resort for fear and uncertainty given its reputation as a safe haven and store of value, the fact that it is non-debasable, and is no one else's liability," said Christopher Louney, a gold strategist at RBC Capital Markets.

Oil prices reached seven-week highs on worries that Iran's exports could decline as the sanctioned OPEC member cracks down on anti-government demonstrations.

Brent gained 1.1% to trade at $64.63 a barrel, while U.S. crude rose 1.2% to $60.22.

data.title: VLSFO firms into wider premium, HSFO discounts little changed
data.description:
Spot differentials for very low sulphur fuel oil (VLSFO) edged higher to a wider premium, compared with the previous day, while discounts for high sulphur fuel oil (HSFO) were broadly stable.

The VLSFO market saw some gains on firmer demand prospects in January from the bunkers sector, with market structure flipping from contango to a mild backwardation.

Premiums for delivered VLSFO bunkers have also inched slightly up recently, some trade sources said.

Meanwhile, HSFO held in discounts as prompt inventories remained ample, with buy-sell indications rangebound in recent trading sessions.

Cracks for both grades were steady to softer on Tuesday.

February 380-cst HSFO/Brent crack (FO380BRTCKMc1) slipped to a discount of $8.90 a barrel at the Asia close, based on LSEG data, while VLSFO crack (LFO05SGBRTCMc1) closed at a premium of $3.15 a barrel.

BUNKER DATA

Marine fuel sales scaled new highs in Singapore during 2025, the Maritime and Port Authority of Singapore (MPA) said on Tuesday, supported by resilient global trade and fuel offtake.

Singapore's bunker sales totalled 56.77 million metric tons in 2025, according to the MPA, surpassing a previous record of 54.92 million tons in 2024.

OTHER NEWS

- Oil prices extended gains on Tuesday as heightened concerns surrounding Iran and potential supply disruptions outweighed the prospect of increased crude supply from Venezuela.

- Vitol and Trafigura have started discussions on Venezuelan crude oil sales with refiners in India and China for cargoes to be delivered in March, several trade sources said.

- Exxon Mobil remains interested in visiting Venezuela and is prepared to send an assessment team there, a source familiar with its strategy told Reuters, a day after U.S. President Donald Trump said he might keep the company out of the country.

- The launch of Indonesia's B50 biodiesel mandate will depend on the price gap between crude oil and crude palm oil, a senior official said on Tuesday.

WINDOW TRADES

- 180-cst HSFO: No trade

- 380-cst HSFO: One trade

- 0.5% VLSFO: One trade

data.title: CAC 40 Drifts Lower; Eiffage, Vinci Among Notable Losers
data.description:
BRUSSELS (dpa-AFX) - France's benchmark stock index CAC 40 drifted lower Tuesday morning in lackluster trading amid a lack of economic or corporate updates. Worries about U.S. President Donald Trump's threat that countries that do business with Iran will now have to pay a 25% tariff on all trade with the United States appear to be hurting sentiment.

The benchmark CAC 40 was down 25.52 points or 0.31% at 8,331.12 a few minutes ago.

Eiffage lost about 3.3%, Vinci drifted down by 3.2%, Saint Gobain lost 2.8% and Thales dropped by about 2.3%.

Bouygues, ArcelorMittal and Engie lost 1.4 to 1.9%, while Veolia Environment, Orange, EssilorLuxottica, Euronext and Stellantis shed 0.8 to 1.1%.

Among the gainers, Eurofins Scientific moved up 1.3%, Bureau Veritas advanced nearly 1% and TotalEnergies climbed up 0.8%.

Pernod Ricard, Legrand, STMicroElectronics, Schneider Electric, Safran, Societe Generale and Michelin posted modest gains.

Airbus shares gained about 0.3%. The company delivered 793 commercial aircrafts to 91 customers in 2025, higher than the 766 aircraft delivered in 2024 and 735 in 2023.

In economic news, data showed France's central government budget deficit narrowed to EUR 155.4 billion at the end of November 2025, from EUR 172.5 billion in the same month a year earlier.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: European High-Yield CDS Costs Fall to 4-Year Low — Market Talk
data.description: The cost of default protection for euro high-yield credit falls to a four-year low as demand for euro credit rises. Demand for corporate bonds remains strong globally, while additionally investors seeking to diversify from the U.S. are buying euro-denominated corporate bonds, pushing risk premiums lower. The iTraxx Europe Crossover index of euro high-yield credit default swaps falls 2 basis points to last trade at 239bps, the lowest since January 2022, S&P Global Market Intelligence data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: DB rearranges ratings on European staples
data.description:
** Deutsche Bank analysts reshuffle their ratings on European consumer staples, downgrading Danone , Haleon and Barry Callebaut while upgrading Beiersdorf and L'Oreal

** The analysts say that while lots of negative factors have been priced in within the sector, the market backdrop still remains difficult, and cut ratings for companies which stock prices over-estimate future growth

** DB expect the consumers equities in 2026 will be most impacted by the shift of sales channel to Amazon, rivalry with niche players, and increasing divide between low-, and high-income customers

** It cites an expected a slowdown in the US health and wellness trend and protein consumption as a reason for the Danote cut

** DB notes Haleon is seen as highly exposed to the channel shift from drug stores to Amazon in North America

** In contrast, it favours the German personal care company Beiersdorf on a likely turnaround and priced-in potential risks

** DB also upgrades French cosmetics maker L'Oreal to "hold" from "sell", noting that potential hit to stock from growth risks seem limited

data.title: ZAWYA-SNG: E-finance submits non-binding offer to acquire non-banking financial services firm
data.description:
Staff Writer

E-finance For Digital and Financial Investments (EFIH) has submitted an indicative, non-binding preliminary offer to acquire 100% of a company operating in the non-banking financial services sector, according to a disclosure.

The offer has received preliminary acceptance from the principal shareholder of the target company.

Following this step, e-finance is expected to submit an application to the Financial Regulatory Authority (FRA) to obtain approval to begin the due diligence process.

The company noted that completion of the potential transaction remains subject to several procedures and approvals.

These include securing the FRA’s approval to commence due diligence, completing the due diligence process to the company’s satisfaction, and reaching a final agreement on transaction documentation.

This is in addition to obtaining final internal approvals from both e-finance and the sellers, and receiving the necessary approvals from relevant regulatory authorities to complete the transaction.

data.title: Brunello Cucinelli Can Outshine Luxury Peers — Market Talk
data.description: Brunello Cucinelli looks a standout luxury stock for the year ahead, Bernstein analysts write after the Italian luxury-fashion house backed its guidance for continued strong revenue growth. Cucinelli expects 10% growth in sales this year after beating analysts' expectations for revenue in the final quarter of last year. The stock looks attractive among luxury peers, Bernstein says, pointing to a sector shift toward the higher end of fashion and a recovery from a share-price fall last year on allegations the company was breaking European sanctions by selling directly to Russian customers. The company is aiming for 1.8 billion euros in turnover by 2028 but could exceed that target, the brokerage says, keeping an outperform rating and a 121-euro target on the stock. Cucinelli shares are largely unchanged at 97.93 euros.(joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 3:30 p.m IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was down 0.30% at 83,627.69, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 index declined 0.22% to 25,732.3 on Tuesday, after fresh tariff threats from U.S. President Donald Trump reignited global trade uncertainty, outweighing optimism around India–U.S. trade talks and quarterly earnings.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee dropped 0.04% versus the U.S. dollar to 90.1900, weighed by a fall in local stocks and regional currencies, while likely intervention by the central bank limited the decline.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN064835G=CC) was quoted at 98.935 rupees, with the yield up 2 basis points at 6.6284%.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was up nearly 2 bps at 5.505%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) rose 4 bps to 5.97%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.55% and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.53%

data.title: Italian Bond Yields Rise Ahead of Heavy Supply and Key Data
data.description:
Italy’s 10-year government bond yield edged higher to around 3.46%, as investors prepared for closely watched economic releases, including US inflation data, amid a busy week of sovereign debt issuance.

Meanwhile, concerns over the Federal Reserve’s independence eased after several Republican lawmakers pushed back against a Justice Department criminal investigation involving Fed Chair Jerome Powell.

In Europe, ECB Governing Council member François Villeroy de Galhau said on Tuesday that expectations of an ECB interest rate hike this year are a “fanciful” misconception.

The ECB left policy rates unchanged in December and revised up some growth and inflation forecasts, reinforcing market expectations that rates will remain on hold for an extended period.

data.title: Diesel structure firms; jet fuel softens further
data.description:
Asia's diesel market structures firmed for a second straight session against a backdrop of firmer ICE gasoil futures despite muted window activity, while jet fuel market structures continued softening.

More spot February offers emerged from Taiwan's Formosa Petchem for 500ppm sulphur gasoil, while some more 10ppm sulphur gasoil offers are expected from the remaining northeast Asian refiners.

On the demand front, Indonesia's Pertamina reissued its tender to procure end-January arrival of 2500ppm sulphur gasoil.

Refining margins (GO10SGCKMc1) extended gains, hitting one-week highs of nearly $19 a barrel, supported by firmness in February paper markets as ICE gasoil futures rebounded.

However, trading activity on the window stayed minimal and cash differentials (GO10-SIN-DIF) rose slightly to premiums of 11 cents per barrel to reflect the higher front-month paper market movements.

Regrade (JETREG10SGMc1) narrowed slightly to 30-35 cents per barrel as Asian jet fuel markets were slightly depressed by a narrower arbitrage price spread with the U.S. West Coast market.

SINGAPORE CASH DEALS

- No deal for gasoil and jet fuel

INVENTORIES

- U.S. crude oil and distillate stockpiles were expected to have fallen last week, while gasoline inventories likely rose, a preliminary Reuters poll showed on Monday.

NEWS

- Vitol and Trafigura have started discussions on Venezuelan crude oil sales with refiners in India and China for cargoes to be delivered in March, several trade sources said on Monday.

- The launch of Indonesia's B50 biodiesel mandate will depend on the price gap between crude oil and crude palm oil, a senior official said on Tuesday.

- Two China-flagged supertankers sailing to Venezuela to pick up debt-paying crude cargoes made U-turns and were headed back to Asia, LSEG shipping data showed on Monday, a sign that the U.S.-blocked South American country might not be directly exporting oil to its main buyer any time soon.

- Brent crude oil's premium to Middle East benchmark Dubai rose on Tuesday to its highest since July as geopolitical tensions in Iran and Venezuela supported the global price marker, LSEG data showed.

data.title: Zalando climbs after Barclays raises to 'overweight'
data.description:
** Shares in Zalando rise about 5% after Barclays upgraded the German online fashion retailer to "overweight" from "equal weight," citing lower risk from AI and agentic commerce

** "On balance we think Zalando scores well and perceived risk is less than feared," Barclays analysts write in a note adding that "valuation makes good entry point"

** In the case of agentic commerce, where AI agents make search and transaction choices for consumers, Barclays analysts say Zalando should be structurally better positioned to defend against possible risks of consumers omitting online retailers than investor narratives imply

** They say it is too early, however, to determine the company an "AI winner" or "AI loser" and expect markets to need a few quarters of evidence showing earnings are not at risk

** Zalando shares fell nearly 22% in 2025

data.title: UBS Group CEO Sergio Ermotti Reportedly To Step Down In April 2027
data.description:
BRUSSELS (dpa-AFX) - UBS Group AG's (UBS) chief executive officer Sergio Ermotti is planning to step down in April 2027, according to a Financial Times report.

However, the report noted that Ermotti could potentially return as the Swiss multinational investment bank's chair in the future.

People familiar with the matter told the agency that the company's asset management chief Aleksandar Ivanovic is one of the executives most likely to become the new CEO.

Notably, UBS's wealth management co-heads Iqbal Khan and Robert Karofsky, as well as chief operating officer Bea Martin are the other contenders for the position, the people added.

In the pre-market hours, UBS is trading at $47.79, down 0.18 percent on the New York Stock Exchange.

data.title: Simon Property Group: Cash-Leasing-Spread Compounding at Scale
data.description:
Introduction

Simon Property Group owns the best shopping real estate in America not the most, but the best. Its portfolio is concentrated in A and A+ malls and Premium Outlets where tenants generate enough sales per square foot to afford rising rents. That sounds simple, but it creates a powerful dynamic: every year, a portion of Simon's leases expire, and because its locations are so productive for retailers, Simon can renew those leases at significantly higher rents. The company doesn't need to build new properties, acquire competitors, or engineer complex financial structures to grow. It just needs to keep signing renewals at higher rates than the expiring leases and let the math compound. That's the entire thesis in one sentence: Simon's rent roll is a compounding engine, and you can watch it work in real time.

KPI

If you only watch one number for Simon, make it this one: the cash leasing spread. When a tenant's lease expires and they sign a new one for the same space, Simon compares the new rent to the old rent. The percentage difference is the cash leasing spread. If a tenant was paying $60 per square foot and renews at $67, that's roughly a 12% spread. It sounds technical, but it's actually the clearest window into Simon's business quality because it collapses everything that matters into a single number.

Think about what has to be true for Simon to raise rents by double digits on renewals. The location has to be productive enough that retailers are making money there. The tenant mix has to be strong enough that shoppers keep coming. Simon's balance sheet has to be healthy enough that it can afford to wait for the right price rather than filling vacancies at any cost. And competitors have to lack equivalent space, so tenants can't simply walk across the street. When the cash leasing spread prints at 10%, 12%, or 14% quarter after quarter, it's proof that all of those conditions are holding.

Simon has been printing spreads in the 1015% range consistently, with recent quarters showing 1213%. That's not a one-time pop it's a pattern that has held through multiple years. As long as that number stays in the double digits and occupancy remains in the mid-90s, the profit engine is working.

Chart 1: Simon's cash leasing spreads have climbed from 8% to consistently above 12% over the past three years.

KPI ? Dollars

Now let's trace how a 12% leasing spread actually flows through to your pocket as a shareholder. This is the bridge from the KPI to returns, and once you understand it, you can update it yourself each quarter.

Start with a realistic scenario: Simon has about 6 million square feet of space rolling over this year, and the expiring rent averages $60 per square foot. If Simon signs renewals at a 12% spread, the new rent is $67.20 per square foot. That $7.20 increase across 6 million square feet creates $43.2 million in additional annual base rent. That's the raw increase now we adjust for reality.

Add about $4 million for "recoveries and percentage rent" industry terms that simply mean tenants also pay their share of common area costs and sometimes a slice of their sales above certain thresholds. Subtract roughly $3.5 million for downtime and free-rent periods (the months between when an old tenant leaves and a new lease starts generating full rent). Subtract another $4 million for the small incremental costs of servicing that space. You're left with about $40 million of additional Net Operating Income, which is the profit the properties generate before corporate overhead and interest. Finally, subtract about $2 million for interest expense and corporate costs, and you arrive at roughly $38 million of FFO growth. FFO Funds From Operations is the standard profit measure for REITs, and it's what supports the dividend. Divide that $38 million by Simon's share count and you get approximately $0.10$0.12 per share of additional annual earnings, purely from lease renewals.

Chart 2: The bridge from leasing spread to FFO showing how $43M of rent uplift becomes $38M of earnings growth.

This math scales predictably. If renewal volume rises to 7 million square feet at 14% spreads, the base rent uplift grows to nearly $59 million, and downstream FFO follows. If spreads moderate to 910%, growth slows but doesn't stop. The KPI tells you immediately which scenario is unfolding no waiting for earnings surprises.

Competition

The same cash leasing spread test reveals why Simon's closest peers struggle to match its economics. Let's walk through each one.

Macerich owns some beautiful coastal malls, but its operational profile is more volatile. Occupancy runs in the low 90s rather than mid-90s, leverage is higher, and the capital structure includes more secured debt tied to individual properties. All of this slows down and dilutes the conversion of spreads into per-share earnings. A 78% spread at Macerich already lower than Simon's has to absorb longer downtime, more expensive tenant concessions, and higher financing costs before it becomes FFO. Simon's unsecured, investment-grade balance sheet means it can hold out for better pricing, reject marginal tenants, and keep more of each spread dollar.

Tanger is the outlet specialist, and it often posts impressive spreads because outlet rents start from a lower base. But Tanger is smaller and more concentrated. When one brand or category stumbles, there are fewer fallback options. Simon's combined regional mall and Premium Outlets platform lets it shuffle concepts across formats moving a successful brand from outlets into malls, or replacing a fading category with fitness, dining, or entertainment. Tanger can match Simon's spreads in good years, but Simon delivers steadier results across cycles.

Federal Realty is the gold standard for open-air, grocery-anchored centers a different format entirely. It runs extremely high occupancy, pristine credit, and tight merchandising. But Federal's cash spreads typically land in the mid-single digits. That compounds nicely given low operating costs, but it illustrates the trade-off: Federal provides downside protection from necessity retail, while Simon offers bigger upside when consumers are healthy. Every point of spread at Simon's higher rent base generates far more absolute dollars of NOI than the same point at an open-air center.

Chart 3: Simon's 12.5% spread leads the peer group and converts more efficiently to FFO.

Chart 4: Occupancy comparison Simon's mid-90s occupancy supports its pricing power.

The bottom line: when you line up the dashboards spreads, occupancy, balance sheet quality Simon's advantage becomes obvious. It consistently posts the highest spreads, converts them most efficiently into FFO, and has the financial flexibility to be patient. That combination is hard to replicate.

Risks

Every thesis has kill conditions, and the virtue of tracking a single KPI is that it turns abstract risks into observable signals. Here's what to watch for.

Tenant sales weakness- If retailers at Simon's malls stop generating strong sales per square foot, their ability to absorb rent increases erodes. The "occupancy cost ratio" rent as a percentage of tenant sales would rise, and Simon would have to accept lower spreads to keep tenants. You'd see this immediately: the cash leasing spread would drop from the 1015% band into single digits, and same-store NOI growth would slow.

Retailer credit shock- If a wave of bankruptcies hits, vacant space increases and Simon has less leverage on renewals. Spreads would compress, and the "waterfall" from KPI to FFO would show larger drags for downtime and concessions. Past cycles have tested this, and Simon's A-quality locations recovered faster than peers but a severe shock would still show up in the numbers.

Interest rate pressure- Higher long-term rates compress REIT valuations across the board and raise refinancing costs. Simon's laddered, mostly fixed-rate debt limits the income statement damage, but the stock price could decline even if fundamentals hold. This is the macro risk that affects multiple over time, not the operating engine itself.

Category shifts- Retail evolves. If certain tenant categories fade and Simon needs to re-merchandise significant space, it can affect the timing and level of spreads. The company has navigated this well converting anchor boxes to fitness, entertainment, and mixed-use but execution matters.

The common thread: each risk ultimately manifests in the KPI. If the cash leasing spread stays in the 1015% range and occupancy holds mid-90s, the engine is intact. If spreads drop persistently into single digits or occupancy slides below 9394%, something has changed. You don't need to guess; you can measure.

Valuation

At recent prices in the low-to-mid $100s, Simon typically trades around 1113 times its next-twelve-month FFO and yields roughly 5% in dividends. That's a modest multiple for a company with this quality of assets and track record reflecting lingering skepticism about malls rather than any flaw in the business.

Here's the base case math. If cash leasing spreads average around 12%, renewal volume stays in the 57 million square foot range at expiring rents near $60 per square foot, and occupancy holds in the mid-90s, FFO per share should grow at roughly 68% annually purely from rent-roll dynamics and modest redevelopment. Add the 5% dividend yield, and you're looking at high-single-digit to low-double-digit total returns without needing the multiple to expand. If sentiment toward retail real estate improves and Simon's multiple moves back toward past-cycle averages, that's incremental upside. But you don't need to bet on it. The returns come from the rent-roll engine doing what it's been doing.

Guru Holdings

Ron Baron (Trades, Portfolio)'s funds own approximately $43.7 million worth of Simon shares (241,000 shares, about 0.12% of his portfolio), purchased at an average cost basis of roughly $152 per share. Baron has made opportunistic trades over time, but recent activity has been net buying: he significantly increased his position by over 300% in Q2 2025, adding 182,000 shares. The stock has traded with considerable volatility, recently ranging between $140 and $171, with an average around $158. Baron's continued accumulation during this period suggests confidence in Simon at current price levels.

Conclusion

Simon Property Group is a compounding machine disguised as a mall landlord. The company owns locations so productive that it can raise rents on renewals by double digits year after year, and because those higher rents flow through to FFO at high margins, the earnings compound without requiring new capital or heroic assumptions. The single number to track is the cash leasing spread it collapses location quality, tenant health, and landlord leverage into one figure you can check every quarter. As long as spreads stay in the 1015% band and occupancy remains mid-90s, the math from leasing to base rent to NOI to FFO per share is straightforward, repeatable, and working in your favor. That's a business you can own patiently.

data.title: PAGE: Gross profit declined, but productivity and cash remained strong; US and Asia led growth
data.description:
Q4 and full-year gross profit declined year-over-year, but productivity per fee earner improved and net cash remained strong. US and Asia showed growth, while Europe and UK faced ongoing challenges. 2025 profit is expected in line with consensus, with further cost savings and AI-driven efficiencies planned.

data.title: Indian shares decline as trade worries spur broad selling pressure
data.description:
By Bharath Rajeswaran

Indian shares slipped in broad-based selling on Tuesday after fresh tariff threats from U.S. President Donald Trump reignited global trade uncertainty, outweighing optimism around India–U.S. trade talks and quarterly earnings.

The Nifty 50 fell 0.22% to 25,732.3, while the Sensex shed 0.3% to 83,627.69.

Both benchmarks rose 0.4% on Monday, snapping a five-session losing streak, after Washington's newly-appointed ambassador to New Delhi said the two countries would discuss trade issues in a call on Tuesday.

"The absence of decisive follow-through buying has kept the broader market outlook guarded," said Dhupesh Dhameja, derivatives research analyst at SAMCO Securities.

Tariff jitters persisted after Trump warned of an extra 25% tariff on countries doing business with Iran.

India's bilateral trade with Iran totalled $1.34 billion in the first 10 months of 2025, with key exports including basmati rice, fruits and vegetables, and pharmaceuticals.

"Global trade uncertainty and India-U.S. trade impasse are adding to near-term market nervousness and delaying a normalisation in foreign fund flows," said analysts led by Bino Pathiparampil of Elara Securities.

Foreign investors offloaded Indian stocks worth $1.72 billion in January so far after a record $19 billion outflow in 2025.

Ten of the 16 major sectors fell. The small-caps rose 0.6% and mid-caps lost 0.2%.

Among stocks, Reliance Industries fell 2.1% after rising 0.5% on Monday. The oil-to-telecom group had dropped 7.4% last week after saying it does not expect any Russian crude deliveries.

Larsen & Toubro fell 3.3% after a report said Kuwait eyed cancelling oil project tenders. L&T, which was the lowest bidder for tenders worth over $4.5 billion, clarified that the projects were not part of its order book.

Online food delivery platform Zomato-parent Eternal gained 3.3% after higher foreign ownership headroom raised expectations of higher weightage in MSCI indexes.

data.description:

data.title: DB says further re-rating not likely for European defence in 2026
data.description:
** Deutsche Bank says a further re-rating for the European defence sector is unlikely in 2026 after its strong 2025 performance, citing regional spending disparities

** The bank downgrades French defence group Thales to "hold" from "buy" pointing to lack of catalysts and budget concerns - it notes that with 20% of its sales exposed, the group is vulnerable to a potential French budget cap

** "While we acknowledge Thales' superior technology offering, we are opting for a more conservative approach, at least for now," says the broker

** It also cuts Italian aerospace firm Leonardo to "hold" from "buy," citing limited upside, adding that "execution is what matters now"

** DB cuts BAE systems to "hold" from "buy" noting the group is unlikely to beat FY25 results expectations off disappointing Maritime margins
